hv/drv_mpipe_intf.h File Reference

Interface definitions for the mpipe driver. More...


Data Structures

struct  gxio_mpipe_notif_group_bits_t
 Bit vector encoding which NotifRings are in a NotifGroup. More...
struct  gxio_mpipe_rules_stacks_t
 Eight buffer stack ids. More...
struct  gxio_mpipe_rules_dmac_t
 A destination mac address. More...
struct  gxio_mpipe_stats_t
 mPIPE statistics structure. More...

Defines

#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_NAME_LEN   32
 Maximum number of characters in a link name.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_DATA   0x00000001UL
 Request shared data permission -- that is, the ability to send and receive packets -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_NO_DATA   0x00000002UL
 Do not request data permission on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_EXCL_DATA   0x00000004UL
 Request exclusive data permission -- that is, the ability to send and receive packets -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_STATS   0x00000008UL
 Request shared stats permission -- that is, the ability to read and write registers which contain link statistics, and to get link attributes -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_NO_STATS   0x00000010UL
 Do not request stats permission on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_EXCL_STATS   0x00000020UL
 Request exclusive stats permission -- that is, the ability to read and write registers which contain link statistics, and to get link attributes -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_CTL   0x00000040UL
 Request shared control permission -- that is, the ability to modify link attributes, and read and write MAC and MDIO registers -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_NO_CTL   0x00000080UL
 Do not request control permission on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_EXCL_CTL   0x00000100UL
 Request exclusive control permission -- that is, the ability to modify link attributes, and read and write MAC and MDIO registers -- on the specified link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_AUTO_UP   0x00000200UL
 Set the desired state of the link to up, allowing any speeds which are supported by the link hardware, as part of this open operation; do not change the desired state of the link when it is closed or the process exits.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_AUTO_UPDOWN   0x00000400UL
 Set the desired state of the link to up, allowing any speeds which are supported by the link hardware, as part of this open operation; when the link is closed or this process exits, if no other process has the link open, set the desired state of the link to down.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_AUTO_DOWN   0x00000800UL
 Do not change the desired state of the link as part of the open operation; when the link is closed or this process exits, if no other process has the link open, set the desired state of the link to down.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_AUTO_NONE   0x00001000UL
 Do not change the desired state of the link as part of the open operation; do not change the desired state of the link when it is closed or the process exits.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_WAIT   0x00002000UL
 Request that this open call not complete until the network link is up.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_RECEIVE_JUMBO   0x010000
 Determine whether jumbo frames may be received.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_SEND_PAUSE   0x020000
 Determine whether to send pause frames on this link if the mPIPE packet FIFO is nearly full.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_RECEIVE_PAUSE   0x030000
 Determine whether to suspend output on the receipt of pause frames.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MAC   0x040000
 Interface MAC address.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_DISCARD_IF_DOWN   0x050000
 Determine whether to discard egress packets on link down.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_POSSIBLE_STATE   0x060000
 Possible link state.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_CURRENT_STATE   0x070000
 Current link state.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_DESIRED_STATE   0x080000
 Desired link state.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MODULE_TYPE   0x090000
 Pluggable module type.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MODULE_EEPROM   0x01
 Module EEPROM contents.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_MAX   3
 mpipe stat configuration command.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_LBL   0x0000
 command = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_LBL config LBL counter.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL   0x0
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL will enable mpipe to collect all packet drop by classifier or loadbalancer.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_ONLY   0x1
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_ONLY will enable mpipe to collect all packet drop by load balancer ONLY.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_BKT   0x2
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_BKT will enable mpipe to collect all packet drop due to bucket full.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_NR   0x3
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_NR will enable mpipe to collect all packet drop due to notifRing full.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_PICK   0x4
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_PICK will enable the mpipe to counts packets assigned to new NotifRing due to sicky-pick finding full NotifRing(or randomized).
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_PKTS   0x5
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_LBL_PKTS will enable the mpipe to collect packet passed throught the load balancer.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_IPKT   0x0001
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_IPKT configs IPKT counter.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT   0x0
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT will enable the mpipe to count packet dropped or truncated due to out of ipkt buffers.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_TRUNCATED   0x1
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_TRUNCATED will enable the mpipe to collect packet truncated due to out of ipkt buffers.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_DROP   0x2
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_DROP will enable the mpipe to collect packet dropped due to out of ipkt buffers.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_PKTS   0x3
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IPKT_PKTS will enable the mpipe to collect packets received from the MAC(s).
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_IDMA   0x0002
 command = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_COMM_IDMA_BE configus IDMA counter.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA   0x0
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BE will enable the mpipe to count packets dropped or truncated due to out of buffer.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_STALL   0x1
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_STALL will enable the mpipe to count number of stall cycles due to waiting for buffers.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_TLB_STALL   0x2
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_TLB_STALL will enable the mpipe to count number of stall cycles due to TLB miss.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_PKTS   0x3
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_PKTS will enable the mpipe to count number of packets written.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BUFS   0x4
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BUFS will enable the mpipe to count number of buffers used.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_RETRIES   0x5
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_RETRIES will enable the mpipe to count number of retried val =s.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_SDN_PKTS   0x6
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_SDN_PKTS will enable the mpipe to count number of SDN packets sent.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_SDN_AF   0x7
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_SDN_AF will enable the mpipe to count number of cycles stalled due to SDN back pressure.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_TRK_AF   0x8
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_TRK_AF will enable the mpipe to count number of cycles stalled due to request tracker back pressure.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_NTF_AF   0x9
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_NTF_AF will enable the mpipe to count number of cycles stalled due to notf-q back pressure.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_SPILL   0xA
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_SPILL will enable the mpipe to count number of buffer stack spills.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_FILL   0xB
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_FILL will enable the mpipe to count number of buffer stack fills.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_EDMA   0xC
 val = GXIO_MPIPE_STAT_CONFIG_VAL_IDMA_BSM_EDMA will enable the mpipe to count number of buffer returned by eDMA.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_10M   0x0000000000000001UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 10 Mbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_100M   0x0000000000000002UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 100 Mbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_1G   0x0000000000000004UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 1 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_10G   0x0000000000000008UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 10 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_20G   0x0000000000000010UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 20 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_25G   0x0000000000000020UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 25 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_50G   0x0000000000000040UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 50 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_12G   0x0000000000000080UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running at 12 Gbps.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_ANYSPEED   0x0000000000000800UL
 Link should run at the highest speed supported by the link and by the device connected to the link.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_SPEED_MASK   0x0000000000000FFFUL
 All legal link speeds.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_LOOP_MAC   0x0000000000001000UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running in MAC loopback mode.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_LOOP_PHY   0x0000000000002000UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running in PHY loopback mode.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_LOOP_EXT   0x0000000000004000UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running in external loopback mode.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_LOOP_MASK   0x000000000000F000UL
 All legal loopback types.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_FDX   0x0000000000010000UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running in full-duplex mode.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_HDX   0x0000000000020000UL
 Link can run, should run, or is running in half-duplex mode.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MODULE_NONE   0
 Link does not have an associated pluggable module.
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MODULE_8079   1
 Link has a pluggable module conforming to SFF-8079 but not SFF-8472 (i.e., does not provide dynamic optical monitoring information).
#define GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_MODULE_8472   2
 Link has a pluggable module conforming to SFF-8472 (i.e., provides dynamic optical monitoring information).

Typedefs

typedef
MPIPE_LBL_INIT_DAT_BSTS_TBL_t 		gxio_mpipe_bucket_info_t
 Another name for MPIPE_LBL_INIT_DAT_BSTS_TBL_t.
typedef uint16_t gxio_mpipe_rules_vlan_t
 A vlan.

Detailed Description

Interface definitions for the mpipe driver.
